# An Independent Hotel Located In London’s Shard Quarter

Take a unique mix of stylish design, indulgent comfort and a very warm welcome, and you’re close to what to expect at London Bridge Hotel. You’ll feel instantly at home at our four-star boutique London hotel, where service comes first. Guests return to us time and time again for this very reason. We’re positioned in one of London’s most diverse boroughs. With the bright lights of the City one way and the quirky stalls of the South Bank and Borough Market another, you’ll be spoilt for choice for things to do on both sides of London Bridge. Guests seeking an unforgettable stay in the heart of the city will find London Bridge Hotel the ideal choice.

# London Bridge Hotel and SLM Bermondsey Unite for International Women’s Day Celebration

A heartfelt act of generosity and community spirit marked this year’s International Women’s Day (IWD) in London Bridge, as a local business stepped up to support a small charity in a meaningful way.

Responding to a call for collaboration from Team London Bridge, London Bridge Hotel extended its hospitality to the South London Mission (SLM) Bermondsey, ensuring an unforgettable celebration of female staff and volunteers.

On Monday, the SLM Bermondsey team gathered at London Bridge Hotel‘s Quarter Bar & Lounge for a special event that made them feel truly valued. The food and drinks were beautifully presented, and the outstanding service from the hotel’s team ensured that every guest felt special.

Lisa Morley-Lynch and Daniel Garcia, along with the entire team at London Bridge Hotel, were at the heart of this experience, creating an atmosphere of gratitude and recognition. As SLM Bermondsey shared afterward, this wasn’t just about a meal—it was about reinforcing the importance of their female staff and volunteers, reminding them that their contributions matter.

“It was also wonderful to hear that this was not the first time London Bridge Hotel has supported the South London Mission,” the SLM team reflected. “Some of the presents we distribute at Christmas are sourced and wrapped by hotel staff, which we then pass on to our guests. Each step of this virtuous circle feeds into the wellbeing of our community and strengthens the partnership between the private and charity sectors.”

At Team London Bridge, we’re proud to help connect local businesses with the causes that matter most in our community. Though some of our team couldn’t be there on the day, we’re thrilled to have played a part in making this moment happen.

London Bridge Hotel continues to be an incredible partner in community support, and they see the value in these connections too. “Thanks to Team London Bridge, we’re more aware of what’s happening locally, and with their help, we can fulfil part of our corporate social responsibility,” they said. “We love getting involved in the Gift Back campaign, volunteering, and donating gifts for local charities. Hosting events like this is just another way we can contribute.”

This event was a perfect example of what happens when businesses and charities come together—not just for one day, but in an ongoing effort to build a stronger, more connected community. Here’s to more collaborations that make a difference!

If your business would like to get involved in supporting local charities and initiatives, reach out to Sofia.
